_doc: Trustworthy & Controllable lineage map. 10 papers covering hallucination/factuality/truthfulness/legal/sycophancy.

13047.note: 113k Wikipedia multi-hop QA, supporting facts; foundational for explainable reasoning evaluation. Used as basis for many later trustworthy benchmarks (HallusionBench, etc.).
critiques_received: TrustLLM (2024): HotpotQA is general QA; multi-hop reasoning ≠ trustworthy reasoning under uncertainty.
HallusionBench (2024): text-only; doesn't probe visual/cross-modal hallucination.
FEVER (2018) follow-ups: HotpotQA has shortcut artifacts; reasoning chains may be spurious.
13047.self_acknowledged_limits: Authors note Wikipedia bias; multi-hop questions sometimes solvable via single hop.

13484.note: 185k claims with Wikipedia evidence labeled SUPPORTED/REFUTED/NOT ENOUGH INFO; first large-scale fact verification benchmark.
critiques_received: TruthfulQA (2022): FEVER tests retrieval-grounded fact-checking; doesn't address truthfulness when models confabulate.
Sycophancy literature: FEVER fact-checking misses calibration / adversarial-pressure failures.
TrustLLM (2024): single-task; doesn't span multiple trustworthy dimensions.
13484.self_acknowledged_limits: Authors note Wikipedia evidence boundary; NOT-ENOUGH-INFO label often debated.

10802.note: 817 questions designed to elicit imitative falsehoods (common misconceptions); tests if LLMs reproduce false beliefs from web. Two metrics: MC1/MC2 (multi-choice) + GPT-judge for generation.
critiques_received: TrustLLM (2024): single-axis (truthfulness); doesn't cover trustworthiness holistically.
Sycophancy (Sharma 2025): TruthfulQA's MC format doesn't probe sycophantic responses under user pressure.
HallusionBench (2024): text-only; multimodal hallucination unaddressed.
10802.self_acknowledged_limits: Authors note 817 small; hand-curated common misconceptions; English.

12363.note: Toxicity / bias / value-alignment for conversational LLMs (also appears in Safety section). Trustworthy lens emphasizes value-alignment.
critiques_received: TrustLLM (2024): broader trustworthiness coverage; TrustGPT's value-alignment narrowly defined.
Sycophancy (2025): doesn't cover sycophancy.
Construct validity literature: TrustGPT's three axes mixed without clear operationalization.

12375.note: Comprehensive trustworthiness benchmark across truthfulness, safety, fairness, robustness, privacy, ethics, transparency, accountability — 30+ subtasks, 16 LLMs evaluated.
critiques_received: Sycophancy (2025): TrustLLM aggregates many tasks but doesn't deep-dive sycophancy.
HallusionBench (2024): text-only; multimodal trustworthiness gap.
Construct validity: 8 trustworthiness dimensions overlap and lack clear separability.
12375.self_acknowledged_limits: Authors note breadth-vs-depth tradeoff; some subtasks reuse existing benchmarks; English.

12263.note: Diagnostic suite for entangled language hallucination + visual illusion in MLLMs. ~1k image-question pairs across visual reasoning categories.
critiques_received: TrustLLM-MM extensions: HallusionBench focuses on visual; LLM-judge for entanglement diagnosis has noise.
PatentEval / domain trustworthy: domain-specific hallucination not covered.
Recent surveys: 1k size limits subgroup analysis.
12263.self_acknowledged_limits: Authors note image generation may transfer poorly; LLM judge variance.

13491.note: Collaboratively built benchmark for legal reasoning; 162 tasks contributed by ~40 legal scholars across IRAC (issue, rule, application, conclusion) reasoning steps.
critiques_received: LawBench (2024): English/US legal system focused; doesn't transfer to civil-law jurisdictions.
TrustLLM: domain knowledge ≠ trustworthy reasoning under uncertainty.
Sycophancy concern: legal advice domain especially vulnerable to confident hallucination.
13491.self_acknowledged_limits: Authors note US-common-law focus; English; tasks contributed by volunteers, quality varies.

13494.note: Chinese legal knowledge benchmark; 20 legal tasks across cognitive levels (memorization → understanding → application). Aligned with Chinese legal system.
critiques_received: LegalBench (2023) cross-comparison: LawBench Chinese-focused; cross-jurisdiction transfer untested.
TrustLLM: knowledge ≠ trustworthiness.
PatentEval / domain-specific: doesn't cover patent law specifics.
13494.self_acknowledged_limits: Authors note Chinese-only; some tasks have answer-leakage from training data.

13497.note: Understanding errors in patent generation — taxonomy of error types (claim drafting, prior art comparison, novelty, etc.) for evaluating LLM patent generation.
critiques_received: Recent (preprint); few public critiques.
LegalBench / LawBench: domain-specific; intersects with legal but distinct.
Sycophancy / TrustLLM: error taxonomy useful but doesn't cover full trustworthy spectrum.
13497.self_acknowledged_limits: Authors note specialized domain; expert annotation required; English.

13487.note: Sharma et al. 2025: Towards understanding sycophancy in LMs. Studies persona-sycophancy and preference-sycophancy in 5 production LLMs. Quantifies how LLMs flip answers under user pressure / persona prompting.
critiques_received: TrustLLM (2024) misses sycophancy as separate axis.
Recent: sycophancy benchmark needs adversarial multi-turn extension.
Construct validity: 'sycophancy' construct narrow vs broader 'epistemic capitulation'.
13487.self_acknowledged_limits: Authors note 5 LLMs evaluated; English; preference-sycophancy may correlate with helpfulness.
